title: AI can understand the world across text, images, audio, video, documents, and space
description: An AI system can combine language with visual, document, temporal, and spatial evidence into one coherent understanding of the task and world state.

subQuestions.title: Can it ground language to visual or audio evidence?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system can connect verbal claims or instructions to the correct parts of an image, screen, or audio trace.
subQuestions.rationale: Grounding performance is already strong enough to support many document, inspection, and interface-centered workflows.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Cross-modal grounding to image and audio evidence
proofItems.shortExplanation: Grounding benchmarks show strong performance on tying language to the right regions, events, and perceptual cues.

subQuestions.title: Can it reason over screenshots, documents, and forms?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can extract and use meaning from dense visual documents and real interface artifacts.
subQuestions.rationale: Document and screen understanding is one of the more deployment-ready multimodal capabilities already visible in practice.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Reasoning over screenshots and forms
proofItems.shortExplanation: Operational audits show strong extraction and reasoning performance on dashboards, forms, and scanned document workflows.

subQuestions.title: Can it combine multiple modalities into one coherent conclusion?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether text, images, charts, and other signals are integrated rather than treated as unrelated fragments.
subQuestions.rationale: Cross-modal evidence synthesis is already strong in many analysis tasks when the sources are legible and the objective is clear.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Multimodal evidence synthesis
proofItems.shortExplanation: Case-based multimodal benchmarks show strong integration of text, image, and chart evidence into unified conclusions.

subQuestions.title: Can it track state across temporal sequences?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can reason about events and changes that unfold over time rather than in a single frame.
subQuestions.rationale: Temporal reasoning is clearly improving, but still lags behind static-image and document understanding.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Temporal state tracking over video-like sequences
proofItems.shortExplanation: Temporal sequence benchmarks show useful progress, but still material misses when key changes are subtle or long-range.

subQuestions.title: Can it solve spatial and diagrammatic reasoning tasks?
subQuestions.description: Measures reasoning over geometry, layout, diagrams, and visually structured state.
subQuestions.rationale: Spatial and diagram reasoning is now strong on many benchmarked tasks, especially when the representation is explicit.

proofItems.title: Leaderboard: Spatial and diagrammatic reasoning
proofItems.shortExplanation: Leaderboard evaluations show top multimodal systems solving a large share of diagram, chart, and spatial-relation tasks.
